steps.title: Find the constant ratio
steps.content:
Since $k$ to $p$ is constant, the ratio $\frac{k}{p}$ remains the same for all pairs of values. Using $k = 7$ and $p = 6$:
$$\frac{k}{p} = \frac{7}{6}$$

steps.title: Apply the ratio to find k when p = 336
steps.content:
Set up the proportion with the constant ratio and the new value of $p$:
$$\frac{k}{336} = \frac{7}{6}$$
Multiply both sides by 336:
$$k = 336 \cdot \frac{7}{6} = 56 \cdot 7 = 392$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
